entries.source_text:
*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
According to Juni, the attack on die Nomaden is not going well. The battle has been ongoing for several days, and Osiris has sustained significant damage, leading the final wave. Juni had to withdraw from the battle to perform emergency repairs in Omicron Alpha. In two hours, we will be back in the war zone.

The mission briefing given to me by Juni did not sound good. Behind that gate a huge Nomad fleet was already waiting for our arrival. 

The first hour of the fights resulted in the destruction of six of our battleships. Altogether, 15 of our battleships, 31 destroyers, and countless gunboats were destroyed meanwhile. The majority of the fighter wings were lost. Even the Osiris had to withdraw from the battle due to necessary repairs.

Eventhough we were able to secure a wider area around the Nomad gate on the other side and destroy many of their ships, we have problems reaching the nearby planets, which probably contain a Nomad colony.
The Rheinland and Bretonian fleets have attempted to ambush Nomad battleships in that area but ran into heavy resistence. The Liberty fleet is guarding the Jumpgate, which is therefore our only secure escape point. Kusari... well, it's likely that the entire Kusari fleet has been lost. As they were pursuing a large Nomad battlegroup, we suddenly lost all communication with them. This is a complete disaster.

Yeah, we made it! The Nomad ships were destroyed, the battle is over!

Wait, what is that?
The sensors discover a substantial energy source on that planet. The output is increasing rapidly... it will explode.

We have to get out of here. Fast!

Two weeks later...


Incoming transmission from the Kusari Fleet: 
"This is Fleet Admiral Yamoto. The Kusari Fleet is under attack... i repeat, the Kusari Fleet is under attack. We need helpppp???@2#?=... a giant nomad fleet is ???ä##... the position of the Jumpgate is in sector B6 in the #,:§!???Lair system. All pilots who can hear my transmission please help us... ?????."

That was all we could receive. It is likely that the debris from the destroyed Nomad Planet is still affecting the signal. It took us almost a week to get back into the X-3043 system without the risk of major system damage. Scouts explored the inner system but were unable to detect any more Nomad activities. The only thing that remains is these massive planetary fragments. However, it has come to our attention that Nomad activity persists in nearby systems. While we are familiar with some of them, the last time we visited them, they were not connected to X-3043. It appears that the Nomads were quite busy during the past year.

It appears that there is a system that holds promise. We will begin our search for the Kusari fleet there.

Juni and King left the Osiris three days ago for Manhattan, but I decided to stay for a while. Orillion hasn't gotten back to me yet.

entries.source_text:
OBJECTIVE:
Enter the Nomad system and conduct a search for the Kusari fleet that was lost. If possible obtain further information about the Nomads.

DIFFICULTY:
Oh great, I've always hated search and rescue missions.

REWARD:
Money and access to new systems.
entries.translation_text:
ZIEL:

SCHWIERIGKEIT:
Oh great, I've always hated search and rescue missions.

BELOHNUNG:
Money and access to new systems.

entries.source_text:
*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
The last mission was a very close call. Fighting Nomaden is one thing, but getting blown up by a massive rock is clearly going too far. I hope to avoid similar situations in the future. We are in the midst of searching for the Kusari fleet. One of the battleships has sent us a signal, which indicates that they have made it through the explosion.

Four months later,...

The information from the data disk shows that the Nomads are expecting something to arrive. It's unclear what this might be, but it might have something to do with a distant galaxy region.

The rescue of the Kusari fleet four months ago has secured the stability around the X-3043 system. The first colony ships started to settle on newly discovered worlds, and soon these worlds will become valuable parts of our civilization.

Looking at the developments of the past few months, everything starts to feel surreal. The data disk from the gunship wreck contained more than just a few information about Nomad Structures. It contained information that the Sol system - our old home - was attacked but not entirely destroyed by the Nomads.
It was surprising to find that this disk had the coordinates of the jump route that the old Sleeperships took.

Bretonia has devoted all their resources to building a path to the old Sol jump route while the Order and other houses secured the new systems.

We are close to finally getting home.

entries.source_text:
OBJECTIVE:
X-3043
In one of the nearby systems, a Bretonian scout made an important discovery.
I am the one who is getting in there to verify the data.

DIFFICULTY:
It should be a straightforward task. Going in, obtaining the data, and leaving again.

REWARD:
Money and FAME (at least that is what Orillion told me).

entries.source_text:
*PERSONAL ENTRY:
I was almost killed during the last mission. But the search for the Kusari fleet has resulted in the discovery of its location. Soon, the other fleets will provide support to these people.
For the time being, I will rest. The analysts are content now.

entries.source_text:
Orillion was right. This will definitely change the world.

What the hell is a Sleepership doing here? All Alliance Sleeperships that were launched from Sol have reached Sirius centuries ago, in more or less good condition.

Hmmm, I remember seeing this insignia before. This vessel belongs to the Coalition!
How is that possible? From where did this ship come?

I have to see if I can get some useful information from the inside of the ship.

OBJECTIVE:
The data disk that was salvaged contained information that the Sol system was not destroyed, and it also had the coordinates leading there. The time has come to return home.

DIFFICULTY:
It's not easy to predict with certainty what we can expect.

REWARD:
Money.

entries.source_text:
One month after the discovery of the Sol system...

Orillion just returned from Sol and took back command of the Osiris while I was on a supply mission close to Planet Crossfire. The Order is building a new station close to Mars but Casper appears to be overly excited about a discovery a Bretonian scout made in one of the systems near X-3043.
I am being send on a "world changing mission", he said.

Well these days that doesn't mean much.
entries.translation_text:

I am being send on a "world changing mission", he said.

Well these days that doesn't mean much.

entries.source_text:
Whoa, that was a wild ride, but we are through.

So this is the Sol System...
I hope it is worth it. 
According to reports, Sol was attacked by aliens and the sun became a supernova, but it seems to be just a fantasy.

Let's investigate the matter at hand.
Scanners indicate no active vessels within close range. Actually, scanners indicate nothing?! No radio signals, no energy sources. This system is practically dead. Only these Jumpgates appear to be functional.

What is going on here?

*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
We have finally found Earth but had to escape after our first visit. It seems like these Nomaden are everywhere and always manage to surprise us. The Nomad Strike Carrier has nearly caused us to lose our lives.

OBJECTIVE:
After waiting for two weeks, our fleets are finally making their way back to Sol. Our objective is to eliminate the Nomad Strike Carrier and all Nomad forces in the proximity.

DIFFICULTY:
Pretty tough.

REWARD:
Money... I almost feel like a merc.
entries.translation_text:
ZIEL:

SCHWIERIGKEIT:

BELOHNUNG:

entries.source_text:
Finally, we are jumping back to Sol.

According to the recon units, the Nomad Strike Carrier has been sighted close to Saturn again. It is time to bring that beast down now.

Our battlegroup will directly engage the carrier. We have to engage all fighters and take out the escorts while our battleships ensure that no alien vessel can escape. When the Nomad Strike Carrier is isolated the second battlegroup will join the fight and take the Carrier out. 
We need to win at all costs.

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
Orillion has returned from Sol.
He was monitoring the construction of a new Order station near Mars, but something more significant must have taken place at this moment. Casper has entrusted me with verifying some data on the Nephele system. *yawn*

One month has passed since the Sol system was discovered...

During my supply mission near Planet Crossfire, Orillion had just returned from Sol and assumed control of the Osiris. The Order is building a new station close to Mars but Casper appears to be overly excited about a discovery a Bretonian scout made in one of the systems near X-3043.
I am being sent on a 'world-changing mission', he said.

Well these days that doesn't mean much.

entries.source_text:
It appears that someone else has found this wreck before. The scanner detects a modern storage container inside. It is classified as a Bretonian military supply case.

Let's inspect what's inside.

OBJECTIVE:
Jack Blinch was hiding something, the question is, 'What?' The Order has been pursuing clues for weeks, ultimately leading us to the Omicron Gamma System. Learn about Blinch's actions here.

DIFFICULTY:
Easy going.

REWARD:
Money... and information.

ZIEL:
Jack Blinch was hiding something, the question is, 'What?' Der Orden has been pursuing clues for weeks, ultimately leading us to the Omicron Gamma System. Learn about Blinch's actions here.

SCHWIERIGKEIT:
Easy going.

BELOHNUNG:
Money... and information.

entries.source_text:
*PERSONAL ENTRY:
Tobias has provided me with assistance in finding clues that could lead me to Jack Blinch. However, I am not completely sure what I am doing here or what to expect. The bars are the best place for me to keep asking for information.

entries.source_text:
Emmergency call!!!

The Rheinland government is requesting urgent help. The contact to a civilian archeological laboratory deep inside the Nomad territory was lost. The weekly report is overdue and all attempts to contact the scientists have failed.

You have to abandon your current mission to find out what is happening there. Further instructions are waiting for you at Freeport 9.

A private company ignored all warnings and started deep field exploration of the unknown system close to the Omicrons. Against all odds, they managed to evade the Nomads and stumbled upon a planet that they called "Lost Paradise".

The surface of this planet is filled with Dom'Kavash ruins and many natural resources, so a small outpost was created there. Until now, we have been receiving weekly reports from the scientists, but we have not received any signals in the last two weeks. The group of scouts that were sent to Lost Paradise never returned.

It's time for someone to go there and find out what's going on. Be careful, the systems are Nomad territory and the jumphole leading to the Lost Paradise system is known to be unstable.

entries.source_text:
OBJECTIVE:
It appears that the research station situated on Planet Lost Paradise is experiencing difficulties. The last contact with the scientist team was three weeks ago. It's important to uncover the truth about what's occurring there.

DIFFICULTY:
High, you have to cross several Nomad systems.

REWARD:
Money.

entries.source_text:
Battleship Hood, three days later....

Now that the civilians at Lost Paradise are secure (at least for the moment), there is another mission waiting.

A small group of large trains need an escort on their way from the Dublin system to the Order headquarters in Sol. As this job leads us close to Jack Blinchs secret coordinates, this escort job it is a welcome opportunity to make some additional money. Juni and King will be waiting for you in Sol.

You can find the convoy leader outside the Hood waiting for you.
entries.translation_text:
Battleship Hood, three days later....

Now that the civilians at Lost Paradise are secure (at least for the moment), there is another mission waiting.

A small group of large trains need an escort on their way from the Dublin system to der Orden headquarters in Sol. As this job leads us close to Jack Blinchs secret coordinates, this escort job it is a welcome opportunity to make some additional money. Juni and King will be waiting for you in Sol.

You can find the convoy leader outside the Hood waiting for you.

entries.source_text:
ZIEL:
While we were on our way to the Sol system, a convoy asked for some security. Escort that convoy from Dublin to der Orden HQ near Mars.
SCHWIERIGKEIT:
Babysitting, nichts out of the ordinary.
BELOHNUNG:
Geld.

*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
Finally back in Dublin. The people on Lost Paradise are all crazy,... sitting between Nomaden and digging in the dust. Well, that's no longer my problem. It's time for me to fly to the Sol system. I have yet to learn a secret.

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
Blinch's coordinates are finally within our reach. I am excited to discover what is so significant. 
Juni is concerned. 
In that area, a small battlegroup's disappeared a few days ago. We need to be cautious.
entries.translation_text:
*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
Blinch's coordinates are finally within our reach. I am excited to discover what is so significant. 
Juni is concerned. 
In that area, a small battlegroup's disappeared a few days ago. We need to be cautious.

Blinch's coordinates are close. Juni and King are following you to learn about the fate of some ships that explored this region but never returned.

DIFFICULTY:
You need to take precautions.

REWARD:
Money... access to new systems.

entries.source_text:
So this is the new Orden HQ.
Impressive how quickly this station was built. With the supplies that our convoy brought to the station, Der Orden will be able to finish the final work on the subsystems.
The new shipyard was the first part of the station that went operational. Der Orden has taken control of the entire system and has secured peace since then.

Jack Blinch's ship

This ship has been damaged. Lifesupport fails to operate. The pilot is dead. There is no information about how this ship was destroyed or who attacked it. The Nomad structures in this system suggest that Blinch encountered a Nomad patrol.

It appears that his journey has come to an end.

Right in front of us is a functional Warp Gate. It would be interesting to know how it got to this place.

*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
I am happy to hear that King is feeling better again. He sustained serious injuries when one of the consoles in his cockpit became overloaded due to a direct hit on his Schiff.
Juni has just returned from Manhattan with a Liberty dreadnought that is meant to guard the Rift. I must hurry to speak with our Koalition guests now.

OBJECTIVE:
We must bring an end to this war once and for all. Mankind is now confronted with even worse foes.
Travel to Altair and get in touch with the Coalition.

DIFFICULTY:
Altair is always a risky place to be.

REWARD:
Money... access to Coalition Systems.

entries.source_text:
The conversation with the Coalition Commander revealed their desire to end the rivalries of the past. Centuries have past since the Sol War, centuries that the Coalition was under siege by the Nomad forces.

Lately, a large number of Coalition ships and defence platforms in their border systems have been attacked and destroyed by unknown forces. The attacks left no survivors or information.
When our ships jumped into Gurm, it appeared logical to the Coalition military that we were the intruders who had caused so much damage to them. So a new war between the Coalition and Alliance started to rise.

The conflicts of the past few months have raised fears that the Coalition could not stand two enemies at once. Meanwhile, the majority of the Coalition military has doubts that we are responsible for the loss of their ships and structures, as both sides appear to be equal in technology and power.

There has to be something else that is out there.

The captain of the Schlachtschiff Deliverance has been ordered to fly the Dimensional Rift and take a defense position there. The Nomad threat from Styx should not be taken lightly.
Der Orden has already dispatched a fleet there, and the construction of three defense platforms is imminent.
Trent and Juni received a request from the Koalition Commander here in Sol to bring the contract for the official ceasefire negotiations to one of their stations, located in the Sovetskaya system. The use of a special transmitter code grants free passage through Koalition space. A demonstration of good will.

Captains log
Jason Brennigan

It's been several years now since the Battle of Crossfire. The loss of the Dyson Sphere and the destruction of X-3043's main planet were a heavy blow for the Nomads. However, the Nomad fleet is still able to hold control of several important systems, such as Death Valley and Freeport 7, which was called this way to honour the victims that died on the first nomad attack. Even after the Houses of Sirius united to fight the nomad thread, we had heavy losses on our side and I have lost many good friends and comrades in the last years. I wish I could go back in time and correct some mistakes.

Wishes... stupid wishes of an old man.

Hey, I should be glad that i got the chance to see our old home, the Earth, instead of dreaming about how my life might have been, if we would have never been attacked by the Nomads. It's likely that I wouldn't have joined the navy again. Well it's right what people say about time... it makes us different from what we wanted to be. It has led me to this place and made me captain of that old, rusted Rheinland Battleship. Well, okay. I can imagine worse places.

At least it seems like the war with the Coalition is over. The leaders of the Houses of Sirius have agreed to a ceasefire with the Coalition. To me, it seems like the Coalition is unable to withstand the heavy attacks from Nomads any longer. After 800 years they can't fight us and the Nomads as once. It's a mystery to me how they managed to survive all these years. For that they have my respect. 

Although I will never trust those Coalition guys I think it is better to forget about the past until the Nomad threat has gone. I just hope for us that they won't stab us in the back if they get a chance. One thing is certain: I will keep my eyes open if a Coalition fighter is near.

The Fleet Command ordered us, the Rheinland battleship Pride of Berlin together with the Rheinland cruisers Victory and Loreley, to the Gladius system to investigate the loss of a small liner transporting our diplomats to the Coalition.

From what we know, they have managed to pass the Nomad systems and were on their way straight to the Coalition headquarters. Gladius was where we had our last contact with them. I wouldn't wonder if a Coalition fighter shot them down, but my opinion is not important. That's why we are here and risk our lives. But who am I to doubt my orders? At times, I think that we are worth less than a piece of scrap metal or a...


*beep*
'Captain we have reached your position and we are receiving unknown transmissions.'
'Coalition or Nomads?'
'No Sir! The transmissions have no known signature'
'What do you mean with no known signature? Who else could be able to do those transmissions if not the Coalition or the Nomads?'
'I have never seen something like this before'
'Ok I'll come. Set a course to Gladius sector E5, we will meet the Victory and Loreley there.'
*beep*


It seems that our 'Phantom' is close. Let's see if it's human or not.

Captains log
EOF

entries.source_text:
Captains log
Jürgen Berngruber

We have lost contact with Captain Brennigan. He wanted to meet us here, said something about an unknown transmission, but now we have been waiting here for hours without any contacts. The Pride of Berlin and the Victory were supposed to be here several hours ago. All we have are weird coordinates that don't make any sense. TX-45, X-76 and KT4OTRWVT-33. It appears to be an old military code. Brennigan was always a friend of those old military codes that were used during the war between the Alliance and Coalition. Maybe he encrypted these coordinates to protect something or someone. God, I hate those war games. 

It's best to locate the nearest Coalition station and request assistance there. It's possible that someone there has knowledge about this code, and perhaps some of the top officers at bars have heard some rumors.

*Alarm, unknown ships approaching*
'What?!?'

Captains log
EOF

entries.source_text:
The conditions for the cease-fire and upcoming peace treaty have been accepted by the Coalition government. At least they keep their word, for now.

The journey to Sovetskaya was mostly peaceful, except for some Nomad patrols, but the wrecks found and their blackboxes are raising concerns.
Who could be so quick and powerful enough to cause so much damage to the Coalition and our ships?

Brennigan's coordinates appear encrypted, but even here on this Coalition station, nobody seems to know the code. One of the equipment dealers advised us to fly to the Coalition headquarters, which is just one jump away. We would be given professional assistance with decryption there.

OBJECTIVE:
Determining what Brennigan's code means and where it will take us is crucial. Perhaps this is the way to identify the force behind the attacks on human colonies.

DIFFICULTY:
Altair is always a risky place to be.

REWARD:
Money... access to remaining Altair systems.

Determining what Brennigan's code means and where it will take us is crucial. Perhaps this is the way to identify the force behind the attacks on human colonies.

SCHWIERIGKEIT:

BELOHNUNG:
Money... access to remaining Altair systems.

entries.source_text:
*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
We have made it to Sovetskaya and the Koalition is keeping its word. I am pondering how long it will last.
We came across two wrecks from Rheinland on our way to this system. We need to identify the cause of their destruction. We have been granted access to all of the Koalition's systems and resources.

One of the Coalition tech specialists was kind enough to help us with the code we retrieved from one of the Rheinland blackboxes.
Even though he is not familiar with this kind of code, he managed to decrypt it for us. He only required the Coalition government to be involved in this matter and to send the coordinates to his superiors as a condition.

We agreed with that.

KT4OTRWVT-33 is the encoded name of a Coalition System close to the Nomad space. TX-45 and X-76 appear to be coordinates in that system.

Gurm, F-7

That's where we'll be going next.

OBJECTIVE:
Captain Brennigan's specified coordinates have been reached by us. This Jumphole leads to uncharted space. Our task is to uncover what lies beyond.

DIFFICULTY:
Highly dangerous.

REWARD:
Money... first contact.

SCHWIERIGKEIT:

BELOHNUNG:
Money... first contact.

entries.source_text:
We have reached the coordinates and suddenly an unknown jumphole appeared in front of us. 

If this is the source of the attacks on our ships then jumping through it will be very risky. However, we have to find out what lies beyond it.
We have no other option.

Hold on, there appears to be an emergency buoy located close to the jump hole. Scan it.

*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
Juni and I have come across a Jumpgate that leads to a system that isn't registered on Star Maps. Even the Koalition is unaware of what lies beyond. I'm unsure if entering without backup is a good idea.

A Koalition fighter wing jumped into this system just at this moment. We are being hailed.
It seems that our friend at the Koalition headquarters has organized a small escort for us. The fighters will catch up in a few moments and form a formation with us.

Armour: 550000
Cargo Holds: 600
Max Batteries/NanoBots: 0/0
Optimal Weapon Class: 10
Max. Weapon Class: 10
Additional Equipment: N/A

The Yggdrasil Battleship symbolizes power and beauty of creation. Innovative design combined with modern technology makes this ship a deadly piece of art.
It is not known where the Yggdrasil Battleship class has its origin. None of the official shipyards ever launched such a ship and apparently it is being sold only to choosen groups. Due to the internal design and structure it is assumed to be produced by a private faction somewhere in Bretonian space.
The bretonian military officially denies any involvement.

This battleSchiff is very slow but its strong weapons and its heavy hull still makes this a dangerous Schiff.

Borderworlds Dreadnought
(Inquisitor-class)

Right from its very appearance on a battlefield, the Inquisitor proclaims its status as a massive weapon, a laser burning through the heart of the ungodly. Everything about it exhibits this focused intent, from the lights on its nose and wings that root out the infidels, to the large number of turreted high slots that serve to destroy them.

Optimale Waffenklasse: 10
Max. Waffenklasse: 10
Zusatzausruestung: N/A
This battleSchiff is very slow but its strong weapons and its heavy hull still makes this a dangerous Schiff.

Rheinland Dreadnought
(Celestis-class)

The Celestis has established itself as one of the most feared and respected battleships around. Since its first appearance almost one decades ago it has seen considerable service in the troublesome regions on the outskirts of the Rheinland territory, helping to expand and defend Rheinland influence there.

Laderaum: 600

This battleSchiff is very slow but its strong weapons and its heavy hull still makes this a dangerous Schiff.

Kusari Battleship
(Navitas-class)

With the Navitas, versatility is the name of the game. Its defensive capabilities make it ideally suited for small raid groups or solo work, while its 10 turret hardpoints present opportunities for untold carnage on the fleet battlefield.

Geschuetze/Tuerme: 0/10
Panzerung: 350000
Laderaum: 600

This battleSchiff is very slow but its strong weapons and its heavy hull still makes this a dangerous Schiff.

Corsair Battleship
(Maelstrom-class)

Recognizing the necessity for a weapon platform to round out their high-end arsenal, the Corsairs brought in top-level talent to work on the Maelstrom. The result: one of the most lethal and versatile battleships ever to take to the dark skies.

Bretonia Dreadnought
(Harbinger-class)

The first Harbinger-class battleship was launched only a couple of years ago, and those that have been built are considered to be prototypes. Little is known of its capabilities, but what has been garnered suggests that the Harbinger is crammed to the brink with sophisticated hi-tech equipment that few can match.

Rheinland Battleship
(Retribution-class)

The Retribution class ship is a tool of destruction. It is designed to enter combat from the outset, targeting enemies at range and firing salvo after salvo at them, and to remain intact on the battlefield until everyone in sight has been torn to shreds.

Kusari Dreadnought
(Manticore-class)

In days past, only those in high favor with the Shogun could hope to earn the reward of commanding one of the majestic and powerful Manticore class dreadnoughts. In latter years, even though now in full market circulation, these golden, metallic monstrosities are still feared and respected as enduring symbols of Kusari might.

Panzerung: 350000

Optimale Waffenklasse: 10
Max. Waffenklasse: 10

This battleSchiff is very slow but its strong weapons and its heavy hull still makes this a dangerous Schiff.

Liberty Battleship
(Iteron-class)

The Iteron is based on Osiris design., the Iteron is the next generation in Liberty Battleships. Able to fire accurately and do tremendous damage at ranges considered extreme by any pilot, this powerhouse will be the bane of anyone careless enough to think himself out of its range.

Thorax


The Thorax is a heavy bomber with limited defence abilities. While it is not advised to run into close combat with this ship, it is however a very effective battleship killer with its six Torpedo mounts.

Catalyst


The Catalyst is fast and agile. It has limited cargo space so it's not very suitable for trading or mining. It is best used as an assault vessel in a hit-and-run type of operations.

Nighthawk


The Nighthawk is a very powerful fighter and can easily tackle the best enemy out there. The Nighthawk has a wide variety of offensive capabilities, making it an unpredictable and deadly adversar.

The Achinisk Station is an isolated hideout of the Koalitions-Rogues. While other stations mainly serve as weapon platform and starting point of assault operations Achinisk is home of thousands of civilians. Its miliary status is weak and the Koalition does not seem to care much about this station.

Koalition spies revealed the existence of a giant Hypergate project located somewhere in Sirius. The construction of an own Hypergate was set to highest priority but kept under the guise of building a new kind of Warpgate. Eventhough the Coaltion was able to create their own hypergate under the correct specifications they never were able to establish a stable connection.

entries.source_text: The Arc is a source of knowledge and research. At least it was before the Dom'Kavash had to abbandon this sector of the galaxy. Guarded by a defense grid this giant monument of power appears to be an unbreakable fortress. Its design raises the assumption that it is older than any other Kavash station we have discovered.

Human, the times are dark. Our situation in the war against the races invading us from other galaxies is debasing.

However, there is a bright hope we can turn the tide in this battle and we need you to fulfill an important part of the task for this hope to become true. The first step will be for you to go to the Smonkves Breeder Station in the Crallard System. There you will find more information about your quest and acquire a key to open the door to a dangerous path and to what may be the breakthrough that may change the destiny of our galaxy.

We have to warn you: You will encounter our former servants on your way. We know the ones you call Nomads are still hostile to Humans despite our collaboration and we are sorry to be unable to alter their behaviour towards your race in a short time period. Their directive to protect our territory is buried deep inside their genetic code and altering it would require to breed a whole new generation and this cannot be done rapidly, We apologize for this.

Welcome human. The Grand Council sent you here so you may help this galaxy survive against the invaders.

Obtain the Crystalline key for the hidden Canis Sector hypergate from the local equipment console .

Once you have acquired this artifact, fly to the Sorror System and locate this gate. Activate it with the Key and get to an abandoned Kavash Colony. For aeons it was the center of our technological research and knowledge but now it is just a relict of old glory . There you will discover a powerful technology we developed a long time ago which use was forbidden due to the dangers of its great might. But now the situation is more critical than ever and it calls for drastic measures if we are going to win the war against the invaders.

A last warning before you go, human. The navigational data we have on this Sector indicates that this region of space is much more dangerous and treacherous than the one you are used to fly in your human colonies. There are many dangers on the way ahead and the Nomads you will encounter are the merest threat in all this.

entries.source_text: The protection of the alien structure is flawless. All Koalition Schiffe were destroyed with ease. As long as we're able to, we should hurry to leave these systems and return to Sirius.

OBJECTIVE:
Visit the Utopia system and reveil why this system is crucial for these aliens.

DIFFICULTY:
The Rheinland military's protection of Utopia means there are no issues to be expected.

REWARD:
Money and information.
entries.translation_text:
ZIEL:
Visit the Utopia system and reveil why this system is crucial for these aliens.

SCHWIERIGKEIT:
The Rheinland military's protection of Utopia means there are no issues to be expected.

BELOHNUNG:
Money and information.

entries.source_text:
*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
Finally, I am back in Sirius. It was about time to find a safe haven again. Juni left in search of Orillion. The Osiris has vanished in the Omicrons and nobody has any idea what happened. I hope Juni will take care of herself out there while I still have to explain how an entire wing of Koalition fighters could be destroyed under our command.

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
The journey to Utopia was relatively uncomplicated, in a way. I am astonished by the sight of this system and the size of the station. Hopefully I will get the answers that I am looking for.

entries.source_text: Inside these massive rock formations, an unknown jumphole appeared. It is not mapped yet. Considering that it is well hidden between the stones and radiation, it is not surprising that nobody has ever heard about it before.

What in God's name... It looks like someone else was here before. This research station is a standard uplink module and it is still transferring data.

Its command module must still be in this system, not far away from here.

The Gas Miners Guild... here?
What exactly is going on here and why is there secrecy?
Expeditions into unexplored space are usually accompanied by large military fleets because we know there are Nomads out there.

The commanding officer of this expedition didn't spend much time on formalities after you arrived at the landing platform. His military tone gives the impression that the GMG is being used as a cover for something bigger. That makes perfect sense as GMG can travel all around Sirius performing operations without drawing much attention.

Before there was even a chance to say no, he gave you orders, just like it was your duty to accept them. As a GMG member, he was well-informed about the person he was talking to and even more about the events of the past few months.

The scans of this system that they took have revealed that it once was home to ancient civilization. Most of the stations and cities were destroyed by Nomad forces centuries ago. Some stations and their automatic defense drones are still operational. The Nomad's main objective was to eliminate all intelligent life forms, just like in Sol. Once that was accomplished, they departed from this system.
The automatic drones possess the key to the Hyperspace technology that Dom'Kavash uses to invade the Altair systems. The signature of their engines have similarities with the scans that were taken from that giant Hypergate in Altair.

Our conventional jump technology and the the more advanced coalition warp technology are restricted to opening natural jumpholes and stabilizing them through the use of gates. New connections can be established between distant regions using this hyperspace technology, where there were no previously existing common connections. Of course, it depends on whether we can control this type of technology.

To determine the functioning of their hyperdrives, it is necessary to scan at least three of these drones. Modifications were made to the scanners to automatically record as much data as possible from the drones. The goal is to get close to them and survive it. The task requires the destruction of three drones.

The GMG commander revealed that there is a secret research station in Omega 3 that requires that information and that some friends will be waiting there.

entries.source_text:
OBJECTIVE:
The GMG is in need of information. Take down three Ancient Drones and travel to the Research station in Omega 3.

DIFFICULTY:
You will face deadly weapon systems, better expect some resistance.

REWARD:
Money, information and access to a new GMG station.

ZIEL:

entries.source_text:
*PERSONAL ENTRY:
Through my exploration, I found a hidden Jumphole leading to a new system. Despite all odds, I came across a GMG Observatory here. Conducting these operations is not something the Gas Miners Guild is known for. Their usual procedure is to wait for military protection before settling in a system.
I was welcomed by their commander with a harsh tone and instructed to go on a special mission. He left the landing pad and walked down a long corridor before I could say anything. I can't shake the impression that he was anticipating my arrival since he was well-versed in my past and the Altair incident.

entries.source_text:
With the scans from the Ancient Drones, the missing parts and the calibration of the nearby Hypergate were completed in just a few days. But how to dial? How to set jump coordinates?
One week passed without any noticeable results. The gate remained inactive.

Until Kusari engineers suggested emitting the frequency that the nomad artifact produced, the same artifact that was used to stop the Nomads at the Dyson Sphere. Suddenly, the gate and the entire station started to vibrate. Power levels exceeded, consoles overloaded, and a massive shockwave took its way through Omega 3.
The EMP destroyed the majority of the station systems and even the Osiris took heavy damage but a Hypergate connection established.

While the most urgent repairs were being done, a small expedition fleet was sent through the gate. The report from these ships is past due...

entries.source_text:
OBJECTIVE:
Pass through the Hypergate and discover the fate of the expedition fleet. In case of radiation damage and the inability to return for repairs, the expedition fleet had a significant amount of repair buoys. Take advantage of them on the other side.

DIFFICULTY:
You are entirely on your own. Be prepared for the worst.

REWARD:
Money, Access to the Inner Core systems.

entries.source_text:
*PERSOENLICHER EINTRAG:
I've finally reached a well hidden station in Omega 3. However, there have been no messages from that KGS Observatory. I was informed that the contact with that station ended shortly after I left that system. It's hard to believe that anyone survived in light of the extensive enemy contacts. I hope this will be worth all the trouble. This station has already suffered enough damage from the Hypergate that is currently in operation. We have to keep our fingers crossed that we have not opened Pandora's box.

The contact with the GMG Research Station was lost right after the jump when the jump tunnel closed behind you. It appears that the Hypergate only opens a one-way connection with its destination coordinates.
Finding scrap metal and building a second Hypergate is the only way back home, it seems.

Ok, maybe that is not the best solution. There might be another way out of here. The expedition fleet obviously faced the same problem, and since there is no debris in the area, they very likely moved on searching for a way back home.

Not far from here is a super massive black hole, so there is no doubt about the current location. This is the center of the Milky Way.
Even from such large distances, some giant structures can be seen high above that black hole. It's a miracle that they can withstand the force of the black hole's gravity. It is highly recommended that you avoid getting too close to them or the black hole. The event horizon would tear every ship apart.

Not far from here, another unknown structure was detected. It is save to go there.

It seems like the structure in front of your Schiff is a small version of a hypergate. Its organic structure and energy signature clearly point to Dom'Kavash's activity in this area of the galaxy. It's surprising that they haven't come to welcome yet.

First scans of this system detected giant asteroid formations with crystalline structures, which are very rare in Sirius and Altair. Long-range scans revealed large structures, which could be stations or something similar. It's difficult to determine what's really out there because these objects are not like any other Dom'Kavash objects we've encountered before.

The sensors also receive multiple readings from repair buoys in this system. This is the direction that the expedition fleet must have taken.

You have been surrounded.
Dom'Kavash station and ships all around you.

... but they don't open fire on you.

Despite sitting right in front of their guns, they keep firing at the other ships coming through the gate.

But why?

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
I am feeling a mixture of fear and loneliness.
It's probable that my arrival at Dom'Kavash station was a surprise for both species.
These stations shed light on the true power of the Dom'Kavash. I'm not sure if I should be glad or anxious at the moment.

entries.source_text:
Unlike other stations, access to this one is not limited to just the hangar. A large door opened shortly after the landing.

A room that could be used as a command module and various other hangars are connected through a wide corridor.

It's time to find out why the Dom'Kavash were eager to meet here.

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
I have the feeling that something strange is going on here. My journey through the Dom'Kavash territory was way too peaceful. Despite this, the majority of the stations are partly restricted and there is no option to land on the planets. It's possible that they're hiding something.

entries.source_text:
Back in Kavash Space it is time to finish this delivery and demand more information about the current situation and how to get back home.

Set course back to the Kavash station where you spoke with your new "friend".

entries.source_text:
Soon after the Far Point One mining base was completed the main investor ran bankrupt. The station was given up just five weeks after completion. 
While most parts of this station are still functional no company showed interest to keep the station running after BMM constructed several large manufacturing platforms in this system. 

Authorities of the Tarsus system speculate that the fast increasing mining operations in Far Point will sooner or later force BMM to purchase the station.

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
Bah, what a desolate area of space. I am wondering what caused the unusual sensor readings throughout Canis Sector. Almost identical to ship's signatures, but vanished when I attempted to take detailed scans.
Well, nevermind. The device is in my possession and I will now head back to my new "friends".

entries.source_text:
CTE -8500 "Kestrel" Civilian Very Heavy


The Kestrel is result of many years of combat experience which influenced the construction of this brand new ship. Based on the combat proven technical scematics of the Eagle, multiple design improvements could be made to create this powerful fighter.

Series ZX "Falchion" Border Worlds sehr schwerer Jaeger
The XZ Series is a new approach to push the Schiff development a bit beyond the current limits. The Falchion has become the new pride of the Border Worlds.

This is the BMM Transport 'Heracles'. We are under attack. I repeat, we are under attack. Our engines are offline and our shields are weakening.

We are surrounded by Nomad fighters.
Our position is in a high orbit around Jupiter. 

To all vessels in range, we are requesting immediate assistance.

Too late. The transport suffered too much damage, leading to a chain reaction that overloaded its reactor core.

Where did these Nomads come from?
And what's up with this gravitational anomaly? It damaged the transports engines, making it an easy target for the Nomads. 

Perhaps there is more to discover.

entries.source_text:
*PERSONAL ENTRY:
I am not sure where the Nomads suddenly came from and where the Battleship's fighter wings went, but it's important for local authorities to be alerted to this incident.
The Sol System is still full of surprises for us.

entries.source_text:
Antares Panzerunged Frachter
The Antares class freigther belongs to a new generation of cargo Schiffe operating in deep space where cargo transports and trains are too vulnerable. Heavily armored the Antares is able to sustain enemy gunfire long enough to either escape or call for reinforcements. Making use of small external cargo pods the Antares is able to hold more cargo than other Schiffe of its class.

A10 "Blackburn" Bomber


The Blackburn is a heavy bomber with limited defence abilities. While it is not advised to run into close combat with this ship, it is however a very effective battleship killer with its six Torpedo mounts. With its reinforced armor plates the Blackburn is the best bomber on the market.

Dragonfly Deep Space Recon Ship

The Dragonfly is a light ship with a powerful engine that allows it to reach speeds that other ships don't even get close to. However, this speed comes at costs of armor and equipment slots. The Dragonfly is a perfect scout ship but lacks combat readiness.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?><RDL><PUSH/><TEXT>A heavily armed defense platform controlled by an onboard threat/response artificial intelligence.</TEXT><PARA/><POP/></RDL>

Decades after the arrival in this system, scientists managed to reverse engineer the Koalition Ion Cannon technology with the help of stolen blueprints. These weapons generate a massive energy output that could destroy even biggest battleSchiffe with just one shot.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?><RDL><PUSH/><TEXT>A Sprungtor allows instantaneous travel between two distinct points in space. Sprungtors were pioneered by Ageira Technologies and are constructed in partnerSchiff with Deep Space Engineering.</TEXT><PARA/><POP/></RDL>
Trade lanes are used for superluminal travel within a system. Trade lanes must be entered from their origin point, usually located near key industrial, military, or civilian bases.

The Éridan is a versatile and sturdy craft that was build to operate in the dense asteroid fields that surround many of the Renaissance stations. Its hull is strong enough to withstand small asteroid impacts.

The La Fayette class Schlachtschiff is the only military Schiff of this size that protects the Renaissance territory. The lack of resources has lead to a very minimalistic but versatile design. Some Schiffe of this class have been refit for special tasks but the default layout usually remained the same.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?><RDL><PUSH/><TRA data="1" mask="1" def="-2"/><TEXT>ALLIES:  </TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="0" mask="1" def="-1"/><TEXT>None</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="1" mask="1" def="-2"/><TEXT> </TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>ENEMIES: </TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="0" mask="1" def="-1"/><TEXT>Nomaden</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT> </TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>Renaissance is a small group of survivors from Earth. During the early days of war between the Alliance and the Coaltion the French fleet was hit hardest. Only a small battlegroup managed to retreat, heavly damaged, after the Koalition's surprise attack on Jupiter's moon, Europa. Over the course of the war multiple attempts were made to reinforce the fleet with new Schiffe and volunteer crews. In the end however, the priorities shifted and the remaining Schiffe of the French fleet were sent to protect the very first SleeperSchiff project. The ANS Aquitaine. The Aquitaine sleeperSchiff prototype and its escort Schiffe went missing without a trace 11 years prior the launch of the five sleeperSchiffe which reached the Sirius sector.</TEXT><PARA/><POP/></RDL>
entries.translation_text: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?><RDL><PUSH/><TRA data="1" mask="1" def="-2"/><TEXT>VERBUENDETE:</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="0" mask="1" def="-1"/><TEXT>Keine</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="1" mask="1" def="-2"/><TEXT></TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>ENEMIES:</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="0" mask="1" def="-1"/><TEXT>Nomaden</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT></TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>Renaissance is a small group of survivors from Earth. During the early days of war between the Alliance and the Coaltion the French fleet was hit hardest. Only a small battlegroup managed to retreat, heavly damaged, after the Koalition's surprise attack on Jupiter's moon, Europa. Over the course of the war multiple attempts were made to reinforce the fleet with new Schiffe and volunteer crews. In the end however, the priorities shifted and the remaining Schiffe of the French fleet were sent to protect the very first SleeperSchiff project. The ANS Aquitaine. The Aquitaine sleeperSchiff prototype and its escort Schiffe went missing without a trace 11 years prior the launch of the five sleeperSchiffe which reached the Sirius sector.</TEXT><PARA/><POP/></RDL>
entries.suggested_text: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?><RDL><PUSH/><TRA data="1" mask="1" def="-2"/><TEXT>VERBUENDETE:</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="0" mask="1" def="-1"/><TEXT>Keine</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="1" mask="1" def="-2"/><TEXT></TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>ENEMIES:</TEXT><PARA/><TRA data="0" mask="1" def="-1"/><TEXT>Nomaden</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT></TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>Renaissance is a small group of survivors from Earth. During the early days of war between the Alliance and the Coaltion the French fleet was hit hardest. Only a small battlegroup managed to retreat, heavly damaged, after the Koalition's surprise attack on Jupiter's moon, Europa. Over the course of the war multiple attempts were made to reinforce the fleet with new Schiffe and volunteer crews. In the end however, the priorities shifted and the remaining Schiffe of the French fleet were sent to protect the very first SleeperSchiff project. The ANS Aquitaine. The Aquitaine sleeperSchiff prototype and its escort Schiffe went missing without a trace 11 years prior the launch of the five sleeperSchiffe which reached the Sirius sector.</TEXT><PARA/><POP/></RDL>

entries.source_text: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16"?><RDL><PUSH/><TEXT>DIAMETER: 8,972 km</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>MASS: 4.78 x 10e24 kg</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>TERRAIN: Desert</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>TEMPERATURE: -70°C to 50°C</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>FLUCHTGESCHWINDIGKEIT: 9.75 km/sec</TEXT><PARA/><TEXT> </TEXT><PARA/><TEXT>Despite possessing a thin atmosphere, this planetoid is rendered uninhabitable by the choking, acrid dust that covers its surface -- in some places to a depth of several kilometers. All survey probes have either been swallowed by the dust oceans or ceased functioning shortly after planetfall, presumably due to the nearly microscopic dust particles working their way into the delicate electronics.</TEXT><PARA/><POP/></RDL>

entries.source_text: The rumors are true. Alien Schiffe have been sighted in our systems. The same Schiffe that also were reported to guard a large structure in the Procyon system. We failed to communicate with them and every contact ended with gun fire. What are they up to? What do they want from us?

Do you remember Earth? Of course not, nobody does. It is a story our parents told us about a world we used to call home. 
For centuries we thought that we would never find this place as we were stranded here without the possibility to escape this barren region of space. 
But now things are changing. Rumours are spreading about a stranger arriving at Alsace. Someone who was not born here.

The scan of the energy signature from the Jupiter Anomaly shows a pattern, similar to an energy burst wave that usually is generated directly before opening a Jump Gate or activating the jump drive engines, that were used on Sleeperships. 

Someone or something tried to jump into this system but something went wrong. Although this energy signature is supposed to collapse within nanoseconds, it remained stable around Jupiter. The Jump Tunnel was never able to stabilize itself.

The size of this phenomenon, which appears to be slowly growing, is even more bizarre. Based on its current growth rate and current size, it appears that the event that initiated this anomaly occurred more than 85 years ago.

It is unlikely to be of Nomad origin. It's remarkable how similar the jump signatures we use are.
Our investigation of the Sleepership we found in Tau Ceti should be a bit more thorough. It was hidden there for a reason. Maybe the Jupiter Anomaly and this Sleepership are part of a bigger puzzle.

entries.source_text:
Not far away the scanner detected the energy signature of an unknown Jump Hole. 

It may be worthwhile to investigate whether it's stable and where it goes. This route could have been taken by survivors if there were any.

Not far away the scanner detected the energy signature of an unknown Sprungloch. 

It may be worthwhile to investigate whether it's stable and where it goes. This route could have been taken by survivors if there were any.

entries.source_text:
Attention: High radiation detected

The double star constellation in the center of the system is causing high radiation pockets in close and medium proximity. It is recommended to avoid these dangerous regions and limit the duration of travel through this system.

It appears that the object is a Cargo Container that is unknown in origin.

The ship that was transporting it had to throw it into space to prevent it from getting stuck in one of the nearby radiation pockets, as it seems.

There was another object detected within a short distance from here.

This battleship belongs to a group known as the Renaissance, which appears to view this region as their home. 

The nearby Ion Cannons might explain why they are surprised to see another human ship coming through this Jump Hole. This is their primary line of defense, nobody else is allowed to cross.

The obvious astonishment when they heard about the Sirius Sector could mean that they never had contact with us before.
Following their orders and explaining the situation in an environment with less guns pointing at your ship may be the best course of action.

entries.source_text:
It must have been a daunting experience for the Renaissance to listen to stories about worlds that were beyond their grasp. At first, they were skeptical of the stories, but they changed their mind when the Jupiter Anomaly was brought up. It appears that they are aware of the cause, but they are reluctant to provide any detailed information at the moment.

It's understandable that they are cautious. 
Their last contact with others was in the midst of a war that was about to end in defeat centuries ago.

The alliance constructed its very first prototype sleepership during the end of the war, which brought these people here and eventually ended up hidden in Tau Ceti. 
During its initial test flight, it vanished. 
Although there was a small crew, the French fleet's remains escorted them. 
Due to an engine failure, it was left stranded in Tau Ceti. 
It was left behind because it couldn't jump back and was too large to move through the dense asteroid fields.
The survivors proceeded with smaller ships, past the hazardous areas of space, but they were unable to travel long distances. A hostile alien race blocked the way forward, destroyed all ships that attempted to pass through their system, and there was no chance of survival on the way back.

The first group of survivors were provided with shelter by a hollow asteroid. The Renaissance began harvesting asteroid fields for minerals and other resources due to the lack of habitable planets in reach. The presence of gas clouds allowed access to oxygen, water, fuel, and other volatile gases. 
Their ability to grow food and sustain life under harsh conditions in this space region was soon accomplished with the construction of new stations.
The Renaissance spread over the relatively limited systems available to them as the population increased.
However, there was a lack of resources, making every year a struggle.

*PERSONAL ENTRY:
I came across something unimaginable. Centuries ago, survivors from Earth found themselves stranded in this region of space. It's difficult to imagine how they managed to maintain their society in such harsh conditions. This region has no habitable planet and only limited natural resources, making it a miracle they've managed to survive for so long.

entries.source_text: Arles Station was built within a mineral-rich asteroid field, which is surrounded by dense gas pockets. The Renaissance's primary mining station is essential for ore extraction, which is crucial for maintaining Schiff construction in the nearby systems.

Orléans Station was the early home of the first settlers in this system. The asteroid offered both shelter and resources. From that point on, Schiffe were dispatched to collect all the necessary items for this very first colony. Although life was harsh, it enabled the Renaissance to survive and even spread to nearby systems.

Orléans Station made an effort to ensure food production in this system. As a result, Bordeaux Station was constructed. Today Bordeaux is the agricultural center of the Renaissance and undisputedly one of the most important stations in this system.

Martinique Station is a focal point for commerce. The majority of transactions with other systems go through this hub. Large storage facilities supply other stations and offer excellent trade opportunities.

The Renaissance fleet's construction relies heavily on the Marseille Werft. This is where the construction of all capital Schiffe and most military fighter craft takes place. Additionally, it functions as a repair facility for any type of Schiff.
Strasbourg Station acts as a military outpost within this system. It serves as a military academy and houses several administrative offices of the Renaissance military and government.

Lyon Station is home to the Renaissance science academy and the special intelligence department. Most information about this station is restricted, and many areas require the highest level of security clearance.
